title: Surface OpenAI synthesis evaluator status in operator plan
problem: Once the OpenAI synthesis evaluator and handoff bridge exist, operator_loop and autocycle need public-safe status fields so operators and agents can see the latest canary verdict, missing gates, artifact path, and recommended next action without running live HTTP automatically.
acceptance_criteria: operator_loop plan JSON includes openai_synthesis_evaluator_status with latest evaluator artifact path, evaluator_verdict, live gates summary, missing gate names, recommended operator command, and whether live HTTP execution is review-gated.
operator_autocycle never runs live OpenAI HTTP automatically and blocks or reports review_gated recommendations for live canary execution.
self_improvement_status includes the OpenAI synthesis evaluator as part of the synthesis handoff spine without exposing secrets or .env.local contents.
When no evaluator artifact exists, plan output recommends the smallest safe mock/injected verification or documents the operator-only live canary command without executing it.
Unit tests mirror existing synthesis_packet_benchmark plan-status patterns and prove dirty-tree/live-gate safety behavior is unchanged.
non_goals: Do not run live OpenAI HTTP from operator_loop, operator_autocycle, execute-safe, or self_improvement_status.
Do not promote draft tickets automatically.
Do not change release governor merge, push, or publish policy.
Do not require OPENAI_API_KEY for default plan/autocycle tests.
rollback_plan: Remove openai_synthesis_evaluator_status from operator_loop, operator_autocycle, and self_improvement_status and revert associated tests. Standalone synthesize --packet and evaluator artifacts remain usable.
